IntegrationStatus.description: Account-level: Configuration status of the Jira integration.

JiraProjectKey.description: Account-level: Jira project key to sync workloads to.

StatusMessage.description: Account-level: Status message on configuration of the Jira integration.

LensSummaries.description: List of lens summaries of available lenses.

NextToken.description: The token to use to retrieve the next set of results.

LensAlias.description:
The alias of the lens.

For Amazon Web Services official lenses, this is either the lens alias, such as serverless, or the lens ARN, such as arn:aws:wellarchitected:us-east-1::lens/serverless. Note that some operations (such as ExportLens and CreateLensShare) are not permitted on Amazon Web Services official lenses.

For custom lenses, this is the lens ARN, such as arn:aws:wellarchitected:us-west-2:123456789012:lens/0123456789abcdef01234567890abcdef.

Each lens is identified by its LensSummary$LensAlias.

NotificationSummaries.description: List of lens notification summaries in a workload.

NextToken.description: The token to use to retrieve the next set of results.

WorkloadId.description: The ID assigned to the workload. This ID is unique within an Amazon Web Services Region.

WorkloadName.description:
The name of the workload.

The name must be unique within an account within an Amazon Web Services Region. Spaces and capitalization are ignored when checking for uniqueness.

LensAlias.description:
The alias of the lens.

For Amazon Web Services official lenses, this is either the lens alias, such as serverless, or the lens ARN, such as arn:aws:wellarchitected:us-east-1::lens/serverless. Note that some operations (such as ExportLens and CreateLensShare) are not permitted on Amazon Web Services official lenses.

For custom lenses, this is the lens ARN, such as arn:aws:wellarchitected:us-west-2:123456789012:lens/0123456789abcdef01234567890abcdef.

Each lens is identified by its LensSummary$LensAlias.

LensAlias.description:
The alias of the lens.

For Amazon Web Services official lenses, this is either the lens alias, such as serverless, or the lens ARN, such as arn:aws:wellarchitected:us-east-1::lens/serverless. Note that some operations (such as ExportLens and CreateLensShare) are not permitted on Amazon Web Services official lenses.

PillarId.description:
The ID used to identify a pillar, for example, security.

A pillar is identified by its PillarReviewSummary$PillarId.

ImprovementPlanUrl.description:
The improvement plan URL for a question in an Amazon Web Services official lenses.

This value is only available if the question has been answered.

This value does not apply to custom lenses.

HelpfulResourceUrl.description:
The helpful resource URL.

For Amazon Web Services official lenses, this is the helpful resource URL for a question or choice.

For custom lenses, this is the helpful resource URL for a question and is only provided if HelpfulResourceDisplayText was specified for the question.

HelpfulResourceDisplayText.description:
The helpful resource text to be displayed for a custom lens.

This field does not apply to Amazon Web Services official lenses.

Choices.description: List of choices available for a question.

SelectedChoices.description:
List of selected choice IDs in a question answer.

The values entered replace the previously selected choices.

ChoiceAnswers.description: A list of selected choices to a question in your workload.

IsApplicable.description: Defines whether this question is applicable to a lens review.

Risk.description: The risk for a given workload, lens review, pillar, or question.

Notes.description:
The notes associated with the workload.

For a review template, these are the notes that will be associated with the workload when the template is applied.

Reason.description: The reason why the question is not applicable to your workload.

JiraIssueUrl.description: The URL of the associated Jira issue.

Metrics.description:
The metrics that make up the consolidated report.

Only returned when JSON format is requested.

NextToken.description: The token to use to retrieve the next set of results.

Base64String.description:
The Base64-encoded string representation of a lens review report.

This data can be used to create a PDF file.

Only returned by GetConsolidatedReport when PDF format is requested.

MetricType.description: The metric type of a metric in the consolidated report. Currently only WORKLOAD metric types are supported.

WorkloadId.description: The ID assigned to the workload. This ID is unique within an Amazon Web Services Region.

MilestoneNumber.description:
The milestone number.

A workload can have a maximum of 100 milestones.

MilestoneName.description:
The name of the milestone in a workload.

WorkloadId.description: The ID assigned to the workload. This ID is unique within an Amazon Web Services Region.

WorkloadName.description:
The name of the workload.

The name must be unique within an account within an Amazon Web Services Region. Spaces and capitalization are ignored when checking for uniqueness.

AccountIds.description: The list of Amazon Web Services account IDs associated with the workload.

AwsRegions.description: The list of Amazon Web Services Regions associated with the workload, for example, us-east-2, or ca-central-1.

NonAwsRegions.description: The list of non-Amazon Web Services Regions associated with the workload.

ArchitecturalDesign.description: The URL of the architectural design for the workload.

ReviewOwner.description: The review owner of the workload. The name, email address, or identifier for the primary group or individual that owns the workload review process.

IsReviewOwnerUpdateAcknowledged.description:
Flag indicating whether the workload owner has acknowledged that the Review owner field is required.

If a Review owner is not added to the workload within 60 days of acknowledgement, access to the workload is restricted until an owner is added.

IndustryType.description:
The industry type for the workload.

If specified, must be one of the following:

  • Agriculture

  • Automobile

  • Defense

  • Design and Engineering

  • Digital Advertising

  • Education

  • Environmental Protection

  • Financial Services

  • Gaming

  • General Public Services

  • Healthcare

  • Hospitality

  • InfoTech

  • Justice and Public Safety

  • Life Sciences

  • Manufacturing

  • Media & Entertainment

  • Mining & Resources

  • Oil & Gas

  • Power & Utilities

  • Professional Services

  • Real Estate & Construction

  • Retail & Wholesale

  • Social Protection

  • Telecommunications

  • Travel, Transportation & Logistics

  • Other

HelpfulResourceUrl.description:
The helpful resource URL.

For Amazon Web Services official lenses, this is the helpful resource URL for a question or choice.

For custom lenses, this is the helpful resource URL for a question and is only provided if HelpfulResourceDisplayText was specified for the question.

HelpfulResourceDisplayText.description:
The helpful resource text to be displayed for a custom lens.

This field does not apply to Amazon Web Services official lenses.

Choices.description: List of choices available for a question.

SelectedChoices.description:
List of selected choice IDs in a question answer.

The values entered replace the previously selected choices.

ChoiceAnswers.description: A list of selected choices to a question in your workload.

IsApplicable.description: Defines whether this question is applicable to a lens review.

Risk.description: The risk for a given workload, lens review, pillar, or question.

Notes.description:
The notes associated with the workload.

For a review template, these are the notes that will be associated with the workload when the template is applied.

Reason.description: The reason why the question is not applicable to your workload.

JiraIssueUrl.description: The URL of the associated Jira issue.

WorkloadId.description: The ID assigned to the workload. This ID is unique within an Amazon Web Services Region.

LensAlias.description:
The alias of the lens.

For Amazon Web Services official lenses, this is either the lens alias, such as serverless, or the lens ARN, such as arn:aws:wellarchitected:us-east-1::lens/serverless. Note that some operations (such as ExportLens and CreateLensShare) are not permitted on Amazon Web Services official lenses.

For custom lenses, this is the lens ARN, such as arn:aws:wellarchitected:us-west-2:123456789012:lens/0123456789abcdef01234567890abcdef.

Each lens is identified by its LensSummary$LensAlias.

PillarReviewSummaries.description: List of pillar review summaries of lens review in a workload.

JiraConfiguration.description: Jira configuration status of the Lens review.

Notes.description:
The notes associated with the workload.

For a review template, these are the notes that will be associated with the workload when the template is applied.

RiskCounts.description: A map from risk names to the count of how many questions have that rating.

NextToken.description: The token to use to retrieve the next set of results.

PrioritizedRiskCounts.description: A map from risk names to the count of how many questions have that rating.

ShareInvitationId.description: The ID assigned to the share invitation.

Lenses.description: The lenses applied to the review template.

Notes.description:
The notes associated with the workload.

For a review template, these are the notes that will be associated with the workload when the template is applied.

QuestionCounts.description: A count of how many total questions are answered and unanswered in the review template.

Tags.description: The tags assigned to the review template.

UpdateStatus.description: The latest status of a review template.

ShareInvitationId.description: The ID assigned to the template share invitation.

LensAlias.description:
The alias of the lens.

For Amazon Web Services official lenses, this is either the lens alias, such as serverless, or the lens ARN, such as arn:aws:wellarchitected:us-east-1::lens/serverless. Note that some operations (such as ExportLens and CreateLensShare) are not permitted on Amazon Web Services official lenses.

For custom lenses, this is the lens ARN, such as arn:aws:wellarchitected:us-west-2:123456789012:lens/0123456789abcdef01234567890abcdef.

Each lens is identified by its LensSummary$LensAlias.

PillarId.description:
The ID used to identify a pillar, for example, security.

A pillar is identified by its PillarReviewSummary$PillarId.

ImprovementPlanUrl.description:
The improvement plan URL for a question in an Amazon Web Services official lenses.

This value is only available if the question has been answered.

This value does not apply to custom lenses.

HelpfulResourceUrl.description:
The helpful resource URL.

For Amazon Web Services official lenses, this is the helpful resource URL for a question or choice.

For custom lenses, this is the helpful resource URL for a question and is only provided if HelpfulResourceDisplayText was specified for the question.

HelpfulResourceDisplayText.description:
The helpful resource text to be displayed for a custom lens.

This field does not apply to Amazon Web Services official lenses.

Choices.description: List of choices available for a question.

SelectedChoices.description:
List of selected choice IDs in a question answer.

The values entered replace the previously selected choices.

ChoiceAnswers.description: A list of selected choices to a question in your review template.

IsApplicable.description: Defines whether this question is applicable to a lens review.

AnswerStatus.description: The status of whether or not this question has been answered.

Notes.description:
The notes associated with the workload.

For a review template, these are the notes that will be associated with the workload when the template is applied.

Reason.description: The reason why the question is not applicable to your review template.

LensAlias.description:
The alias of the lens.

For Amazon Web Services official lenses, this is either the lens alias, such as serverless, or the lens ARN, such as arn:aws:wellarchitected:us-east-1::lens/serverless. Note that some operations (such as ExportLens and CreateLensShare) are not permitted on Amazon Web Services official lenses.

For custom lenses, this is the lens ARN, such as arn:aws:wellarchitected:us-west-2:123456789012:lens/0123456789abcdef01234567890abcdef.

Each lens is identified by its LensSummary$LensAlias.

PillarReviewSummaries.description: Pillar review summaries of a lens review.

Notes.description:
The notes associated with the workload.

For a review template, these are the notes that will be associated with the workload when the template is applied.

QuestionCounts.description: A count of how many questions are answered and unanswered in the lens review.

NextToken.description: The token to use to retrieve the next set of results.

ShareInvitation.description: The updated workload or custom lens share invitation.

ShareInvitationId.description: The ID assigned to the share invitation.

ShareResourceType.description: The resource type of the share invitation.

WorkloadId.description: The ID assigned to the workload. This ID is unique within an Amazon Web Services Region.

LensAlias.description:
The alias of the lens.

For Amazon Web Services official lenses, this is either the lens alias, such as serverless, or the lens ARN, such as arn:aws:wellarchitected:us-east-1::lens/serverless. Note that some operations (such as ExportLens and CreateLensShare) are not permitted on Amazon Web Services official lenses.

For custom lenses, this is the lens ARN, such as arn:aws:wellarchitected:us-west-2:123456789012:lens/0123456789abcdef01234567890abcdef.

Each lens is identified by its LensSummary$LensAlias.

WorkloadId.description: The ID assigned to the workload. This ID is unique within an Amazon Web Services Region.

WorkloadName.description:
The name of the workload.

The name must be unique within an account within an Amazon Web Services Region. Spaces and capitalization are ignored when checking for uniqueness.

AccountIds.description: The list of Amazon Web Services account IDs associated with the workload.

AwsRegions.description: The list of Amazon Web Services Regions associated with the workload, for example, us-east-2, or ca-central-1.

NonAwsRegions.description: The list of non-Amazon Web Services Regions associated with the workload.

ArchitecturalDesign.description: The URL of the architectural design for the workload.

ReviewOwner.description: The review owner of the workload. The name, email address, or identifier for the primary group or individual that owns the workload review process.

IsReviewOwnerUpdateAcknowledged.description:
Flag indicating whether the workload owner has acknowledged that the Review owner field is required.

If a Review owner is not added to the workload within 60 days of acknowledgement, access to the workload is restricted until an owner is added.

IndustryType.description:
The industry type for the workload.

If specified, must be one of the following:

  • Agriculture

  • Automobile

  • Defense

  • Design and Engineering

  • Digital Advertising

  • Education

  • Environmental Protection

  • Financial Services

  • Gaming

  • General Public Services

  • Healthcare

  • Hospitality

  • InfoTech

  • Justice and Public Safety

  • Life Sciences

  • Manufacturing

  • Media & Entertainment

  • Mining & Resources

  • Oil & Gas

  • Power & Utilities

  • Professional Services

  • Real Estate & Construction

  • Retail & Wholesale

  • Social Protection

  • Telecommunications

  • Travel, Transportation & Logistics

  • Other

Notes.description:
The notes associated with the workload.

For a review template, these are the notes that will be associated with the workload when the template is applied.

RiskCounts.description: A map from risk names to the count of how many questions have that rating.

PillarPriorities.description: The priorities of the pillars, which are used to order items in the improvement plan. Each pillar is represented by its PillarReviewSummary$PillarId.

Lenses.description:
The list of lenses associated with the workload. Each lens is identified by its LensSummary$LensAlias.

If a review template that specifies lenses is applied to the workload, those lenses are applied to the workload in addition to these lenses.

ShareInvitationId.description: The ID assigned to the share invitation.

TrustedAdvisorIntegrationStatus.description: Discovery integration status in respect to Trusted Advisor for the workload.

WorkloadResourceDefinition.description:
The mode to use for identifying resources associated with the workload.

You can specify WORKLOAD_METADATA, APP_REGISTRY, or both.

Applications.description: List of AppRegistry application ARNs associated to the workload.

PrioritizedRiskCounts.description: A map from risk names to the count of how many questions have that rating.

JiraProjectKey.description: Workload-level: Jira project key to sync workloads to.

StatusMessage.description: Workload-level: Status message on configuration of the Jira integration.

WorkloadId.description: The ID assigned to the workload. This ID is unique within an Amazon Web Services Region.

SharedWith.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID, organization ID, or organizational unit (OU) ID with which the workload, lens, profile, or review template is shared.

WorkloadName.description:
The name of the workload.

The name must be unique within an account within an Amazon Web Services Region. Spaces and capitalization are ignored when checking for uniqueness.

WorkloadId.description: The ID assigned to the workload. This ID is unique within an Amazon Web Services Region.
